skills.name: Vitest — Fast Unit Testing Setup
skills.description: Vitest is the preferred test runner for Vite-based projects and works natively with TypeScript, ESM, and React. Drop-in Jest API replacement.

skills.name: @testing-library/react — Component Testing Patterns
skills.description: Testing React components by simulating user behavior rather than implementation details. Queries by role, label, and text — not CSS selectors.

skills.name: MSW — Mock Service Worker for API Mocking
skills.description: MSW intercepts HTTP requests at the network level using Service Workers (browser) or Node.js interceptors (tests). Tests see real fetch/axios calls, no mocking of modules.

skills.name: Playwright — End-to-End Testing
skills.description: Playwright E2E tests run against a real browser. Covers critical user flows: auth, checkout, form submission, navigation.

skills.name: Testing Custom React Hooks with renderHook
skills.description: Test custom hooks in isolation using @testing-library/react renderHook, avoiding component wrappers.

skills.name: Accessibility Testing — jest-axe and Playwright Audit
skills.description: Automated a11y checks using jest-axe for component tests and Playwright's accessibility snapshot for E2E flows.

skills.name: Visual Regression Testing with Playwright Screenshots
skills.description: Capture and compare visual snapshots to catch unintended UI changes across commits.

skills.name: Test Coverage Configuration and Thresholds
skills.description: Configure Vitest coverage with V8 provider, enforce thresholds by folder, and generate reports for CI.
skills.template:
# Test Coverage Configuration

## vitest.config.ts — Full Coverage Setup
```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    coverage: {
      provider: 'v8',
      include: ['src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
      exclude: [
        'src/**/*.stories.{ts,tsx}',
        'src/**/*.d.ts',
        'src/test/**',
        'src/mocks/**',
        'src/**/index.ts',  // re-export barrels
      ],
      reporter: [
        'text',        // terminal output
        'html',        // browsable report in coverage/
        'lcov',        // for Codecov/Coveralls upload
        'json-summary', // for GitHub PR comment bots
      ],
      thresholds: {
        lines: 80,
        functions: 80,
        branches: 70,
        statements: 80,
        // Per-file thresholds:
        perFile: true,
      },
    },
  },
});
```

## package.json Scripts
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:ci": "vitest run --coverage",
    "coverage": "vitest run --coverage && open coverage/index.html"
  }
}
```

## GitHub Actions Coverage Report
```yaml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: npm run test:ci

- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
  with:
    file: coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

## What 80% Actually Means
- 80% LINE coverage = every module's happy path is tested
- 70% BRANCH coverage = most if/else branches exercised
- Focus on business logic and edge cases, not getters/setters
- Use `/* c8 ignore next */` sparingly for genuinely untestable platform branches

skills.name: Vitest Mocks, Spies, and Stubs (vi.*)
skills.description: Vitest's vi.* API replaces jest.* for mocking modules, functions, timers, and environment variables.

skills.name: Testing Strategy — What to Test and Where
skills.description: Practical guide to the testing pyramid for modern web apps: when to use unit, integration, component, and E2E tests.
skills.template:
# Testing Strategy

## The Modern Web Testing Pyramid
```
         [E2E - Playwright]        ← Few, slow, critical paths
       [Integration - MSW + RTL]   ← Medium count, realistic
     [Component - RTL + Vitest]    ← Many, fast, behavior-focused
   [Unit - Vitest]                 ← Many, fastest, pure logic only
```

## What to Unit Test (Vitest)
- Pure functions: formatters, validators, transformers, calculators
- Custom hooks (no UI needed)
- Business logic isolated from framework
- Error handling and edge cases

**NOT unit tests**: components with external dependencies, anything that needs DOM

## What to Component Test (RTL + MSW)
- User interactions: form submission, button clicks, navigation
- Conditional rendering: loading, error, empty, success states
- Accessibility: keyboard nav, ARIA, focus management
- Components that fetch data (mock network with MSW)

## What to E2E Test (Playwright)
- Critical user journeys: sign up, log in, checkout, publish
- Cross-page flows: auth gates, redirects, persistence
- 3rd party integrations: Stripe, OAuth
- Mobile vs desktop layout differences

**NOT E2E**: error message copy, hover styles, unit-level logic

## Practical Ratios (per feature)
```
Unit:       5-10 tests  — data transforms, validators
Component:  3-5 tests   — render states, key interactions
E2E:        1-2 tests   — happy path + one critical error path
```

## Test File Conventions
```
src/
  utils/
    formatDate.ts
    formatDate.test.ts     ← co-located unit test
  components/
    LoginForm/
      LoginForm.tsx
      LoginForm.test.tsx   ← co-located component test
e2e/
  auth.spec.ts             ← E2E in separate top-level dir
```

## Red Flags in a Test Suite
- All tests pass but app is broken → testing implementation not behavior
- Tests break on refactor without behavior change → too much detail tested
- E2E tests for every edge case → push to unit/component level
- Zero MSW usage with API-dependent components → over-mocking modules
